title: Ordered Hierarchical Target Priors For Strategy Context
summary: The data has very high-cardinality strategy identifiers (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, compound/stint combos).
rationale: The data has very high-cardinality strategy identifiers (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, compound/stint combos). Public encoder docs show cross-fitted or ordered target encoding is specifically meant to keep this signal without catastrophic leakage. This is a more concrete extension of simple frequency features and should be stronger than global means because pit timing is highly conditional on race, compound, and stint context.
implementation_hint: Build leak-safe encodings in chronological order within each training fold for keys such as `Driver`, `Race`, `Race_Year`, `Race_Compound`, `Driver_Compound`, plus hierarchical fallbacks like `Driver->Driver_Compound->Driver_Compound_Stint` and `Race->Race_Compound->Race_LapBucket`. Export both posterior and posterior-minus-parent-prior features. Compare CatBoost native categoricals vs explicit ordered encoders.
expected_effect: Small but realistic CV lift if current model is underusing categorical structure; biggest gain should be on rare drivers/races where hierarchical shrinkage beats one-hot/frequency counts.
risk: Easy to leak if encodings are not strictly fold-local and time-ordered; public LB may drop if rare-category priors overfit historical race identity.
